sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Good, RIGHT worked. The platform moved from cols 34-38 to cols 39-43. Let me check the current position and see if I can move UP from here.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
ascii_grid = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
# Find orange/blue platform
platform_positions = []
for r in range(15, 55):
    for c in range(64):
        if ascii_grid[r][c] in 'Ob':
            platform_positions.append((r, c))
if platform_positions:
    rows = [p[0] for p in platform_positions]
    cols = [p[1] for p in platform_positions]
    print(f"Platform: rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Check what's above the platform
print("\nAbove platform (cols 34-45):")
for r in range(24, 35):
    row_str = ascii_grid[r][32:46]
    print(f"  Row {r}: {row_str}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, the platform is at rows 30-34, cols 39-43. Above the platform:
- Row 24: ccGGGGGccccccc (corridor area)
- Rows 25-29: GGGGGGGGGGGGGG (gray structure)
- Rows 30-34: platform
The platform is at cols 39-43, and above it at rows 25-29 is all gray (G). So the platform can move UP through the gray structure.
Let me try moving UP to see where the platform goes. The platform is at rows 30-34, so UP would move it to rows 25-29, then 20-24, then 15-19, etc.
But wait, when I moved UP from rows 30-34 earlier (when the platform was at cols 34-38), it went to rows 15-19 and got stuck. Let me check if the platform at cols 39-43 can move UP differently.
Actually, looking at the corridor area (rows 17-24, cols 33-37), the platform at cols 39-43 is to the RIGHT of the corridor. So if I move UP, the platform might go through a different path.
Let me try UP and see what happens.
